_provenance.query: MATCH (a:Method)<-[r1:CALLS]-(b:Method) WHERE a.file_path IN ['src/main/java/es/name/restaurants/application/port/repository/CourseRepositoryPort.java'] AND a.name = 'save' AND r1.confidence >= 0.55 RETURN a.file_path AS a_file, a.name AS a_name, b.name AS b_name, b.file_path AS b_file, r1.confidence AS r1_conf LIMIT 200
_provenance.note: Callers-side (inbound) anchor pinned by the INTERFACE file (CourseRepositoryPort.java) + name ('save') per R5/design §4.2's callers-side resolution clause: the anchor file_path here is the BODYLESS interface's own file, and the query still yields the real caller (createNewCourse, in the impl file) because CALLS edges attach to the interface-owned Method node for inbound resolution -- this is the documented non-anchor-hop asymmetry (the impl-anchoring concern in §4.2 is specifically about resolving OUTBOUND traversal to the impl node; inbound (callersOf) is unaffected because the caller always calls the interface-declared method signature).
